target.name: AIR
target.founder: Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, Netanel Goldberg

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
AIR is a Series A transportation company founded by Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, and Netanel Goldberg that has raised $23M to date. The transportation sector comprises 8 identifiable private peers—including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger—with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using AIR's $23M total raised as a baseline proxy for post-money valuation, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $46M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 5 same-sector transportation companies exist, none of the refined peer subsets (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier) produced the minimum of 3 datapoints needed for reliable cross-sectional benchmarking. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given sector specificity constraints.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $39M (low) to $53M (high), centered on a $46M median. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer comps in the same stage and valuation tier, limiting confidence in the stage-progression multiple itself, and the lack of any public market reference points. Further validation would require access to recent financing rounds among closer stage-and-sector peers or operational metrics (revenue, growth rate) to anchor revenue or margin multiples.
